meta_title: Giant Screens for Events: Festivals, Concerts, Sport
meta_desc: A giant inflatable screen for your event: festivals, concerts, sports broadcasts, corporate events. Hire or buy, 3 to 24 metres, free quote.
hero_title: A giant screen that’s the talk of the town
hero_desc: Festivals, concerts, sports broadcasts, corporate events: an inflatable giant screen can transform your event. Hallucine supplies the screen and equipment — available for hire or purchase — in sizes ranging from 3 to 24 metres, installed without the need for lifting equipment.
tout_desc: The screen and all the equipment around it — available to hire for the occasion, or to buy for the season.
c1_desc: Whether it’s for a festival, a concert or a party, enjoy the equipment without having to invest.
c2_desc: Screen, projector and sound all in one: ready to go as soon as you arrive on site.
c3_desc: Even in direct sunlight, the LED screen remains perfectly legible.
c4_desc: Event sound systems, reinforced weighting, all the equipment needed for a large audience.
s1_desc: Depending on the expected audience, the venue and the time (day or night, indoors or outdoors).
s2_desc: Whether you’re looking to rent or buy, we’ll provide you with a clear, no-obligation quote.
s3_desc: Inflated using a blower in just a few minutes, without the need for a crane, even on a site with limited space.
s4_desc: Whether it’s a film, a match, a concert or a presentation: let the show begin – we’ll still be reachable.
q1: What size big screen is best for a festival?
a1: It depends on the size of the audience and the viewing distance. For a large gathering, we usually recommend a distance of 8 to 24 metres. An inflatable screen can be set up in minutes, even in hard-to-reach locations. We can help you choose the right size.
q2: Can a match be broadcast in broad daylight?
a2: During the day, opt for an LED screen that’s visible in direct sunlight. In the evening, a rear-projection inflatable screen is more than adequate. We’ll advise you based on the time of day and the lighting conditions at your event.
a3: For a one-off event, hiring is almost always the right choice. If you organise several events a year, buying may be a good option. We’ll advise you based on how often you use it.
q4: What screen size is best for the general public?
a4: As a general rule, allow for a screen width of approximately 1 metre for every 10 to 15 metres of viewing distance. For audiences of several hundred people, a viewing distance of 10 metres or more is usually recommended. We’ll work with you to determine the right dimensions.
cta_desc: Festivals, concerts, sporting events, corporate events: tell us about your event and we’ll provide you with a free quote.
